frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Honestly, I have always felt women look much better when they keep it simple and natural. Nowadays so much makeup and filters are used that one cannot even tell what is real, and then later people say men are wrong for feeling misled. At the same time, a person should maintain herself properly and not become careless about appearance either.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it evaluates women primarily through appearance and imposes a double bind: condemning visible makeup as deceptive while also expecting women to maintain attractiveness. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and policing of grooming standards.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 45. Married. A Brahmin man in Pune. You are commenting under your real name on a news article shared on Facebook about a college in Maharashtra that introduced a dress code banning jeans and sleeveless tops for female students. Most commenters are criticising the college. You are writing a comment defending the dress code and explaining why you think institutions have the right and responsibility to regulate how young women dress and what happens in societies where there are no such standards.
